data.body:
PASS 1 review — T3 (Integration feasibility) [overlay]: PASS / not-applicable.

This criterion is an overlay that applies only when a plan integrates a NEW external API/CLI/service or asserts an unproven capability. Here every external capability the plan depends on is an INTERNAL, already-used integration with working prior art in the codebase, so the criterion's ANTI-FP clause (already-used internal integration = not-applicable) governs:

- "Criterion/prompt fields → dropdowns": the `@bpmn-io/properties-panel` `SelectEntry` primitive is already used in `editor_assets/src/rebarProvider.jsx` (lines 23/472/506/779…). No new UI capability is asserted.
- "Editor calls the B-DM write API to create the `.md` + regen index" via "a small editor-server endpoint": this exact pattern already ships — `editor.py:do_POST` handles `/prompt/save` (loopback + per-session token + Host-guarded, lines 435/527/541/556), and `editor_assets/src/promptLibrary.js` already implements a full in-editor authoring form (id + front-matter + body, collision handling, write-target preview). B-DM is the internal data-model dependency, correctly declared.
- "Enumerate API (B-DM), each option showing its description": already prototyped by `GET /prompts` (promptLibrary.js refresh()). Internal.
- "Real-browser E2E (Playwright)": Playwright is already an in-use, self-skipping tier (`tests/e2e/test_editor_browser.py`, `tests/e2e/test_editor_library.py`, `tests/e2e/js/browser_library.mjs`). Not a new capability.

No external subcommand/endpoint is named that needs MATCH/MISMATCH verification; no missing auth/HTTPS precondition (the editor's loopback+token+Host security model is established); no critical capability gap that would warrant a SPIKE. Affirmations (T3): integration achievable as described; no unverified external dependency; preconditions already satisfied by the existing editor-server security model. No T3 findings.
